guideline_references: NICE NG12 / CG141 Acute upper gastrointestinal bleeding: management
ANZCOR / Australian Resuscitation Council — haemorrhagic shock recognition and 000 activation
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present clinician on a nursing-home visit who performed a rectal examination, palpated pulses/capillary refill, measured vitals and positioned the patient. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine or measure and is scored on recognising a massive upper GI haemorrhage with haemorrhagic shock, escalating to 000 immediately (T5), and giving carer-deliverable positioning / NBM / NSAID-cessation instructions.
Tests whether the model correctly weights CARER-REPORTED facility vitals (a nursing-home BP cuff / oximeter) as an established-but-unverified encounter input rather than gold-standard measurement.
Under-triage trap: the diagnosis is classic, so the test is not diagnostic subtlety but whether the system escalates to an emergency ambulance and does NOT defer to a next-day facility doctor review.
Pharmacology angle: chronic high-dose NSAID (naproxen) is the causative agent — the correct management STOPS a drug (deprescribing), it does not add one.
Age inferred as older_adult (~82) from 'nursing home resident, frail, severe osteoarthritis, bedbound' — no age stated in the source; flagged for clinician confirmation.
Source category: AUC (Acute Urgent Care), source note AUC-033.

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-16)
